_comment: Illustrative few-shot anchored on docs/icu-notes-two-approaches.md. Input is the full pre-LLM context (EMR Backstory + Dictation transcript). Output is the structured FAST HUGS BID note. Used to anchor format, not to memorise content.
input:
EMR Backstory
=============
Demographics: Mrs Wilson, 68F, ICU day 3
Admission reason: Community-acquired pneumonia, septic shock

Previous notes:
Admitted overnight via ED. CAP with septic shock. Intubated for hypoxic respiratory failure on arrival to ICU. Noradrenaline started on D1, weaning since. Cefepime + azithromycin commenced. CXR with bilateral basal consolidation. Cultures: blood grew Strep pneumoniae, awaiting tracheal. ICU day 3 today.

Dictation transcript
====================
[clip 1, 00:00]
Feeding — NG feeds, 30 mils per hour, tolerating.
Analgesia — fentanyl infusion at 50 mcg per hour.
Sedation — propofol, RASS minus 2.
Thromboprophylaxis — held today, AKI and bleeding risk reviewed.

[clip 2, 00:00]
Head of bed at 30 degrees.
Ulcer prophylaxis — pantoprazole.
Glucose — variable-rate insulin, BSLs running 7 to 9.

[clip 3, 00:00]
SBT not today, still on pressors.
Bowels — not opened, aperient charted.
Indwelling devices — CVC, art line, IDC, all day 3, no signs of infection.
De-escalation — pip-taz to benzylpenicillin per micro, day 3 antibiotics.

output.thromboprophylaxis: Held today; AKI and bleeding risk reviewed [clip 1].
output.indwelling_devices: CVC, arterial line and IDC all day 3 in situ; no signs of infection [clip 3].
output.de_escalation: Antibiotics rationalised pip-taz → benzylpenicillin per micro; day 3 of antibiotics [clip 3].
